title: Legal acceptance vs consent prompt vs review before submit vs checkbox group vs confirmation dialog vs typed confirmation vs age gate
summary: Use legal acceptance when a user must expressly accept terms, conditions, contracts, policies, or disclosures before access or a transaction; use consent prompt for optional data-use opt-in, review before submit for checking captured answers, checkbox group for ordinary multi-choice input, confirmation dialog or typed confirmation for action-risk prevention, and age gate for eligibility by age.
decisionRules: Choose legal acceptance when the user must manifest agreement to terms of service, conditions of sale, service agreement, acceptable-use policy, workplace terms of use, contract amendment, or required legal disclosure before proceeding.

Choose consent prompt when the decision is optional data use, marketing, research participation, AI training, partner sharing, personalization, or sensitive-data processing; do not hide optional consent inside legal acceptance.
Choose review before submit when the user checks their own captured answers before committing a transaction, not when the key task is accepting a legal document or policy version.

Choose checkbox group when the field is an ordinary short set of independent choices; a legal acceptance checkbox is a contract or policy gate with document version, required state, audit record, and blocking behavior.

Choose confirmation dialog when the user already invoked a consequential command and must confirm intent; legal acceptance is agreement to a standing rule or transaction term, not just a one-time consequence check.

Choose typed confirmation when severe scope requires reproducing a target phrase such as a repository name; legal acceptance should not make users type I agree unless the agreement risk specifically needs a stronger acknowledgement.
Choose age gate when eligibility depends on age, age band, or parent authorization; age gate may appear before legal acceptance but it does not replace acceptance of terms.
A legal acceptance flow must name the document, version or effective date, owning party, scope, required action, linked or embedded full terms, and consequence of declining.
Do not rely on continued browsing, footer links, hidden scroll boxes, prechecked boxes, disabled Continue buttons without errors, or bundled marketing consent as evidence of legal acceptance.
For updated terms, show what changed, whether acceptance is required now or after a grace period, what access is blocked, and where the acceptance record can be audited, downloaded, or printed.
failureModes: A checkout says By paying you agree to terms, but the terms link appears only in a footer after payment is submitted.
A required checkbox is prechecked, so the user never performs a clear acceptance action.
Terms acceptance also signs users up for marketing, analytics, and partner sharing.
An updated workplace policy blocks access with no document version, effective date, change summary, or decline route.
The UI requires scrolling through a tiny embedded legal document but provides no download, print, search, or language alternative.
The acceptance record stores only true with no user, document, version, timestamp, locale, or source surface.

sources.note: Supports checkout terms-of-service agreement as a required acceptance before payment.
Supports checkbox text, full terms content, checkout placement, and required agreement enforcement before order placement.
Supports resource-access terms of use, device or recurring acceptance, PDF document presentation, and acceptance reporting.
Supports clear and conspicuous disclosure, proximity, prominence, and material transaction terms.
Supports clickwrap as a button or checkbox acceptance pattern distinct from full signature workflows.
